title: Signal Dry Bulk Weekly Report
summary:
Main
The first week of May signals a soft rebound in freight rates with Capesize demand in ton days posting signs of revival. In the iron ore market, prices for iron ore cargoes with a 63.5% iron ore content for delivery into Tianjin rebounded towards $14/t. The current level has not been seen for over a week and seems that the current euphoria stems from China's new policy measures to avert a slow economic growth for this year.

children.title: Main
children.summary: The first week of May signals a soft rebound in freight rates with Capesize demand in ton days posting signs of revival. In the iron ore market, prices for iron ore cargoes with a 63.5% iron ore content for delivery into Tianjin rebounded towards $14/t.

children.title: SECTION 1 - FREIGHT - Market Rates ($/t) - Firmer
children.summary:
‘The Big Picture' - Capesize and Panamax Bulkers and Smaller Ship Sizes
Image reference: unnamed (16).png | assets/2022-05-04_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed281629_92a68aaa111d.png
The freight sentiment emerged firmer for the first days of May, however, the current signs of revival remain below the levels seen five weeks ago. Capesize Brazil-to-North China freight rates held levels around $26/t, while the last peak was during Weeks 10 and 11 at levels of $29/t.

children.title: Number of Vessels - Increasing
children.summary:
Supply Trend Lines for Key Load Areas
Image reference: unnamed (17).png | assets/2022-05-04_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed281729_9ba0aa53a70c.png
The number of ballasters signals a sharp increase in the Supramax and Handysize segments for the last two weeks, while in the Capesize, there are still levels below the one-year average. Capesize SE Africa: The number of vessels sailing in ballast posted a slight drop to around 66 vessels, 6 vessels less than the previous week with a 20% decrease from the last peak of Week 11.

children.title: Decreasing
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (18).png | assets/2022-05-04_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed281829_2313e45a9903.png
The overall trend of demand ton-days posted a further decrease since the last week, with signs of revival only seen in the Capesize segment since Week 14. Capesize demand ton-days: There is a remarkable growth with levels nearing the days of the last peak during Week 12.

children.title: Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
section_path: Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.section_path_text: Dry bulk ships congested at Chinese ports
children.summary:
Image reference: unnamed (19).png | assets/2022-05-04_signal-dry-bulk-weekly-report_img_unnamed281929_883e72e76990.png
Dry bulk ships in congestion sustained increased levels but with a softer pace than last week's spike. Overall, the Handysize, Panamax, and Supramax segments posted the highest levels during the last three weeks compared to the ending of Week 14.
